4428724:
Dockside Giant

The Dockside Giant is a massive copper statue located in Katapesh's Docks district that depicts a bearded man with horns and a large trident staring menacingly out to sea. The statue has been there since at least 2589 AR and is seen by the locals as a ward against bad weather and bad luck. The locals must constantly battle against the elements to keep the statue clean and decent.

There are various theories as to who or what the statue depicts. Some believe it depicts an ancient hero, while others believe it is a now-dead deity of the sea. Whatever it depicts, the Dockside Giant has become a landmark of the Docks.
17070494:
Androsphinx of Zukebri

The Androsphinx of Zukebri is an ancient monument in Osirion's Underdunes in the form of a sphinx, with a bronze-cast face of an unknown pharaoh and massive ruby and sapphire adornments. Powerful magic protects it from the Underdunes' constantly shifting sands and leaves it at the center of a perfectly circular crater of flat ground, and any who approach it are assaulted by its piercing telepathic screams similar to mind quakes. The monument also seems to weep tears of unknown black fluid.

A cult of Lamashtu, comprised of the rare people unaffected by its telepathic attacks and possibly inspired by another Lamashtan cult near Eto, worships the monument and has sacrificed travelers who draw near. Occult researchers have attempted to link the androsphinx to the 11 gifts reportedly given to the Four Pharaohs by "Emissaries" from Aucturn in the Calosian Manuscript.
41793104:
Damaq Palace

The Damaq Palace lives up to its name, for this ostentatious structure rivals many palaces of Inner Sea kings, its facade frequently redecorated simply because House Damaq tires of seeing the same thing everyday. The amount of wealth possessed by the Absalom's noble family is simply incalculable to the common plebeian mind, and although the decoration has remained static for decades, some saying it is in mourning for Aroden and others that it is a sign that the family's wealth is drying up, Scion Lord Kerkis pays no attention to these unfounded rumors.
48463214:
The Smoke Lady

The Smoke Lady is a black and purple shop where the Smoke Lady tells fortunes for travelers for a fee. Children can be heard outside advertising, and inside, the woman is hidden behind a smokescreen that obscures her face as she foretells the destiny of the clients.

58830174:
Bastardhall

The ruined fortress of Bastardhall, originally called Arudora Manor or Castle Arudora, is located on Lake Laroba near the West Sellen River on the eastern border of the Ustalavic county of Varno.

Bastardhall is best known in Ustalav for the black coach that leaves it once a century in search of seven people descended from the Arudora family, which are brought back to Bastardhall for unknown purposes. The internal workings of Bastardhall are obscured from the outside world: the castle is perpetually shrouded in fog, and attempts to magically probe its interior reveal a veil of powerful holy magic warding the interior of the castle's island. The only contact the castle makes with the rest of the world occurs once a century, when the fog parts to let the black coach pass.
59005314:
Thicketfell

Thicketfell is a forest near the Tors of Levenies in the western part of the mountains. The region is completely uninhabited, with few traces of civilization, although a small community of gnomes lives in the center of the grove, while to the south, a group of cyclopes survive in the ruins of their civilization. Many creatures inhabit the area, from rocs and dragons that dominate the heights to fey that dwell in the forest.

93004984:
Beragel's Fur and Feathers

Beragel's Fur and Feathers is a pet shop run by Beragel Tindertales, offering animals from all over Golarion, as well as food and specialized enclosures to keep them happy. As a rule, sentient animals, endangered species, and monsters are not sold in the shop, though, whether by mistake or due to her gnome tendencies for novelties, she sometimes acquires a larva or hatchling that later turns out to be something that shouldn't be anyone's pet.
99374654:
Concerned Residents' Union

The Concerned Residents' Union is Eastgate's second council, unrecognized as official. However, because the district is such a close-knit community, decisions made by the residents are often final, while official recognition is merely a formality. Their headquarters are located in a red brick building in the district center, and landowners and shopkeepers coordinate their schedules to meet regularly. The union's operations could be considered organized crime, as they decide who to elect as Nomarch, which lieutenants to be promoted to the Post Guard, and what favors the council owes to the union's businesses.

The organization operates through donations to charitable causes, though this money tends to disappear as it passes through various channels, and any criminal investigation is terminated before it even begins, simply by one resident whispering something to another until the guard catches on. The union isn't interested in lining its own pockets; it aims to maintain the calm and supportive atmosphere of Eastgate, even if it means marginalizing some people. The two cults, the Children of Spring and the Circle of Stones, are a concern, as President Gelda Dellby's son, Rance, has joined the Children as a recruiter. This betrayal has hardened her heart, and she seeks to close the park and eliminate the cults. The more sons and daughters who join the cults, the more likely her proposals are to be approved.
108043184:
Urgir

Urgir is the unofficial capital of the orc-ruled Hold of Belkzen. The nation has no true capital as Belkzen has no centralized government, yet the ruler of Urgir is generally seen by most orcs as the most powerful chieftain in the area. The disparate tribes of Belkzen are fiercely independent and fight against each other even more than they fight outsiders. Urgir's leader as of 4724 AR, the intelligent Ardax the White-Hair, has instituted changes in the hopes of encouraging cooperation and greater unity.
118184444:
Odd Stories

Odd Stories is a bookshop located at 100 Roseguard Road in Otari, on the Isle of Kortos. Its proprietor is the human wizard Morlibint.
121369324:
Mount Spire

At almost 16,500 feet, Mount Spire is the highest peak in the Menador Mountains. The Veil of the Spire falls more than 300 feet into a large pool that is the origin of the Yolubilis River. Bellezeph Villa caters to an elite clientele of tourists who come to admire the falls, try out their mountaineering skills, or engage in amateur spelunking. Behind the falls are extensive networks of caves, home to cave fishers, chardas, goblins, and kobolds.
136451614:
District Courthouse

The District Courthouse in the Precipice Quarter, like many parts of the district, was abandoned following the earthquake that shook the city. The judges sealed the building with barricades after wrongfully executing Jarbin Mord, and his spirit is said to haunt the white marble halls of this crumbling four-story building.

Seven's Sawmill

151956674:
Oxjaw Falls

The Oxjaw Falls is a 40-foot-tall cataract formed by the Crook River east of the Slave Trenches of Hakotep, near the headwaters of the river. The Crook drops suddenly from the highlands into the lower reaches of the Brazen Frontier region of Osirion. Oxjaw Falls create a moist and verdant pocket within a rocky grotto. The lizardfolk that live at the base of the cataract worship and guard an ancient altar set into the grotto's mossy walls. At the top of the cataract the water flows through deep channels between flat rocks (resembling the ox's teeth) that allow a slippery crossing for travelers on foot. It is the only safe foot crossing west of the Shallows of Ipeq and has the advantage of being away from the Ipeq guards and gossipy fisher folk.
155935454:
Gottacker

Gottacker is a small community located on one of the tributaries of the Profit's Flow, inhabited by exiles from the Five Kings Mountains. The settlement is therefore a pleasant destination for those seeking to rewrite their past after making mistakes from which there is no return.
159559614:
Sleeping Dove

The Sleeping Dove is a shrine dedicated to Sarenrae located in the western reaches of central Katapesh, less than 100 miles east of Solku.

The history of Sleeping Dove is not a happy one: over 100 years ago, a group of pilgrims dedicated to Sarenrae were on a pilgrimage to Solku when they were ambushed by monstrous bandits—some say kholo, while others say jackalweres—and were slaughtered. A week later the bodies were found by passing travellers, who saw their tattered white gowns blowing in the wind and thought they looked like doves.

Several paladins from Solku came out to the site and built a shrine to Sarenrae here to remember their fallen brethren. Ever since the shrine of Sleeping Dove has been sacred ground. Many a traveller stops to rest by the shrine as it seems to repel evil creatures and has even granted mysterious, and sometimes prophetic, dreams to good and devout creatures.
165442854:
Kantaria

The Chelaxian town of Kantaria stands just west of the Sedna River, between the Barrowood and the Menador Mountains to the north. It is the capital of the Archduchy of Menador.
176339084:
Frostfell Manor

Frostfell Manor serves as a residence for Irriseni dignitaries while visiting the Nidalese capital of Pangolais, particularly the Jadwiga. The building is constructed of pale stone and crystal, and a cold atmosphere pervades the grounds. Most of the locals frown upon displays of wealth and Nidalese–Irriseni relations are frosty, thus the mansion is completely isolated from the surrounding neighborhoods.
178367004:
Slave Barge Dock

The Slave Barge Dock is the most used port on Parego Regicona, although in 4710 AR a group of erinyes took control of it to use in their search for new victims.
178935844:
Alendruan Harbor

Founded by the elven witch called Kaala Raingold around 4622 AR, Alendruan Harbor is as of 4712 AR the main port on the Shackles' island of Kepre Dua.
180328274:
Second Labyrinth

The Second Labyrinth is a popular social and gambling club among chelaxians and gamblers in The Coins district, run by the beautiful Lady Azoria. The gothic exterior and gargoyles watching passersby are the first things one sees upon arrival, and inside, games of all kinds from Vudra to Riddleport fill the tables. Since Lord Gulv's return, the establishment has been experiencing a resurgence, and the profits earned by House Tevineg are on the rise.
182714234:
Temple of Asmodeus

The temple of Asmodeus in the Varisian city of Korvosa's Heights neighborhood is a star-shaped building with a relatively small number of priests.
186272804:
Menador Keep

Menador Keep is a two-story dwarven-engineered gatehouse at the narrowest point of the Menador Gap, which connects the Chelaxian Archduchy of Menador to Ravounel through the Menador Mountains. The keep forms a short, artificial tunnel that bottlenecks traffic, which House Thrune staffs with loyal soldiers who exert heavy taxes and strict regulations on travel.

During the Glorious Reclamation insurgency of 4716 AR, Menador Keep was commanded by Lucian Thrune.
202934634:
Windwatch Keep

Windwatch Keep is a castle in the Mindspin Mountains near Molthune's western border with Nidal. It is the home of the Windwatchers, a sect of seers devoted to the god Gozreh.
207597354:
Adrast

The Barony of Adrast is a small barony and settlement in the north-west of Taldor, located north of the Verduran Forest and south of the westernmost mountains of the Fog Peaks in the Northern Tandak Prefecture. The Baron of Adrast is Baron Stelan who is married to Baroness Lenelle. The headsman of Adrast settlement, a small village, is Odric, and the captain of the guard is the dromaar Captain Drelm. Following the baron's professed faith, the predominant religion is that of Abadar, and the barony has a cleric and temple to that god.
209688414:
Mother Sphinx

Mother Sphinx is a shop a few blocks from the Grand Bazaar where Horner Shan sells household gadgets at great prices. Behind secret doors, illusions, and traps in the underground lies a temple dedicated to Norgorber, where Horner is its high priest, praying to the Gray Master aspect of the deity. Rival gangs gather to settle disputes, recruit new talent, or profess their faith, but few know that Horner knows what goes on beneath his shop or that he is the leader of the city's most secretive thieves' guild, the Smoke Knights.
210295464:
Fort Landing

Fort Landing is an ancient, ruined stone fortress on the western coast of the Isle of Terror in Lake Encarthan. The fort has crumbled over the centuries and is now little more than a series of interconnected walls that surround a partially collapsed tower covered in arm-thick ivy. The true name of the fort has been lost to time, and its current one stems from the fact that the white sand beaches near the ruin are the easiest place to land when approaching the island from the west. The ceaselessly blowing wind blows against the fort's western wall, sometimes forming sand drifts as high as 30 feet. The nightwing Jolanera actively avoids Fort Landing as part of an agreement made with the Whispering Tyrant.

215354834:
Ashkurhall

Ashkurhall is an ancient abandoned dwarven settlement in Mount Nakyuk, located in a jungle vale of the Mwangi Expanse known by as the Valley of Broken Blood. It was the subject of the final expedition by the Pathfinder Society Venture-Captain Nieford Sharrowsmith, which led to his death.

253642434:
Ravenscry

Ravenscry is a basalt statue in the Atteran Ranches depicting a raven with outstretched wings. It is said that a tribe abandoned the faith of Zon-Kuthon for the spirit faith, and that when the Kuthites learned of this, they sacrificed them on the altar in front of the statue, which they left as a reminder for future generations. The statue has attracted the attention of Adannos Blackraven, patriarch of his tribe, who was seeking ways to protect his son, who was being drawn to a Desnan cult and thus attracting the attention of the Umbral Court. The statue communicated with him, offering advice on how to help his son.

The truth is that the one giving the advice is a vilderavn, who seeks to bring ruin to Adannos's tribe, just as he did in the past with the first tribe he tempted.
272161834:
Wetan

Wetan was a lirgeni city that was swallowed by the waters when the Eye of Abendego opened. A tribe of athamaru found the ruins in the Abendego Gulf and claimed them as their territory. They began to disrupt the city's magical defenses until they reached an observatory with copper plates bearing strange symbols. When they activated the arc within it, fiendish energies lashed out at them. The once peaceful tribe roams the region, attacking anyone who approaches, and the malevolent energies continue to spread throughout the waters of the gulf.
272219924:
Mirnura

Mirnura was once the capital of Jistka Imperium, founded by the legendary hero Arustun. The city was named after Arustun's beloved wife. It was situated on what is now the northern coast of Rahadoum, on the Fallen Peninsula north of the Path of Salt.

Mirnura was the main target in Ancient Osirion's campaign to destroy the Jistkan empire. When Osirians burned the city to the ground, they used millions of tons of salt to eradicate all signs of life around it. The Path of Salt region was named for this epic razing.

Powerful sandstorms sometimes reveal hints of monuments, and the vaults of Jistkan emperors are thought to show themselves to individuals of royal Jistkan descent. One of these vaults, known among the treasure hunters as the Halite Crypt, lies in tremendous depths and is rumored to still be protected by a Jistkan behemoth golem by the name Helatoros.
283505534:
Castinlee

Castinlee is an agricultural settlement located in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos. It is known for the many orchards of productive fruit and nut trees in the area.

304965344:
Irim

Irim is a city of aquatic elves hidden in the continental margin off the northern coast of Ravounel and west of Nidal in Nisroch Bay.
306134544:
Kintargo

Kintargo, also known as the Silver City, is the capital of Ravounel and is situated on Nisroch Bay at the mouth of the Yolubilis River. It is the main harbor for ships heading north to Varisia and west to Anchor's End.
328928044:
Fort Ramgate

Fort Ramgate is a frontier town of the nation of Molthune, in the Marideth River valley. It has impressive fortifications, which are tested on an almost daily basis by the forces of neighboring Nirmathas. In fact, the town takes its name from the repeated attempts to destroy its gates. Despite this, the town's population continues to grow, as Molthune's Imperial Army concentrates its forces here in preparation for the almost inevitable invasion of Nirmathas. Indeed, most of the few elite troops of the Imperial Army are currently stationed in Fort Ramgate.

The town is commanded by General Hakar. General Lord Resper Cyrno ostensibly rules the Border region of Molthune from Fort Ramgate as its territorial seat, but spends very little time there.
339461614:
Learned Pig

The Learned Pig is one of the most popular taverns in the Wise Quarter. It doesn't follow the trend of affiliating with any single educational institution, preferring to remain neutral and welcoming students from all walks of life to sample its strong drinks. The exterior is decorated with flags and symbols of various district institutions. It's a roughly square building, and inside, the space is chaotic, with bookshelves overflowing everywhere and tables surrounding the central hall. People come from all walks of life to join in scientific collaborations and political or philosophical debates and when a debate is undecided, all eyes turn to the eponymous Learned Pig, who sits on a pen behind the tavern and awards the victory to whoever goes first when offered food. Upstairs, dignitaries can sleep in comfortable rooms, although unfortunately, the floorboards don't block out the noise from conversations below, making it difficult to get a good night's sleep.
342903234:
Labyrinth of the Two Hells

The Labyrinth of the Two Hells is an extensive series of caves under the northern Zi Ha mountains that is home to two tribes of fiendish xulgaths. The Gugrangmyal are the "children of the cold hell" while the Gutshadmyal are the "children of the hot hell", and both equally brutal groups are in a perpetual war against the other. The war, nurtured by the samsaran Baijan who manipulated the xulgaths' spiritual personas, has persisted for centuries.
353423264:
Falchion Point

367594424:
Grotto of St. Elth

The Grotto of St. Elth is a small shrine dedicated to Sarenrae in a grove near the Arthfell Forest. The body of Elth Hammerson is buried in the grotto, which was made a holy site by the High Priest of Sarenrae in Almas after his sacrifice saving lives. Once a year, on the anniversary of his death, he manifests and those who have visited him notice that he appears increasingly melancholic.
372071984:
Fort Liberthane

Fort Liberthane is the only settlement in the tiny River Kingdom of Liberthane founded by the revolutionary "Young Knight" Achille Parsall in 4689 AR. The small fortress was the base of operations of a notorious bandit before Parsall and his followers seized control of it. The fort is home to approximately 50 soldiers. A number of farming families have settled around the fort.
377310394:
Crescent Cove

Crescent Cove is a large bay that sits along the northern coastline of the Holomog province of Gwamne and the south-eastern coast of Zanjalia province. Crescent Cove is renowned for the helpful merfolk that live there, enjoying the waters of the Obari Ocean. These merfolk are willing to help sailors by granting them boons and even helping them repair their ships. Once every seven years, the merfolk of Crescent Cove choose a "sea-groom" from amongst the Obari pirates and a "land-bride" from amongst the residents of Holomog's coastal provinces, though, despite the names, either can be of any gender. The two people selected are thereafter blessed with fair weather and good fortune, but must spend the next seven years living with the Crescent Cove merfolk, though this is far from a hardship.
382127884:
Crystalcrag

Crystalcrag is a town of geniekin in the remote southern Zho Mountains of Qadira. Due to prejudices against geniekin, the town is protected by illusions created by Tygora Cinderfury, an ifrit exile from Medina Mudii'a.
386724634:
Vargas Swamp

Vargas Swamp is the largest swamp in the predominantly dry country of Katapesh, spanning almost 30 miles in length and half that in width. Located in north-central Katapesh, myriad trees, herbs, and grasses grow there, attracting herbivores and their predators. A small tribe of lizardfolk makes its home here as well.

Vargas Swamp also contains numerous ruins and the skeletons of old villages (such as Slither Cove), that were swallowed by the rising water levels, which can fluctuate unpredictably. Some blame this strange occurrence on a green dragon said to live in the swamp, although this has never been proven.
403978154:
Tanglegate

Tanglegate is an elf gate in the demon-corrupted forest of Tanglebriar. It can be reached via Lichgate, which is the only known example of a working elf gate that links to Tanglebriar. Its portal key is a sapphire.

Treerazer, the lord of Tanglebriar, hopes one day to be able to use Tanglegate against the elves, and so he has ordered his minions to refrain from destroying it. The gate itself is a stone arch covered in moss, and it stands on a rare patch of dry ground in the middle of a stinking bog.
413311344:
Latchmin's Folly

1 locations:
  • F & M's Exotic Meats
414769644:
Tilxar

Little is known about Tilxar, a location in northeastern Geb near the Ydrion River.
418697504:
Gerig's Workshop

Gerig's Workshop is where the popular drink of Gerig the Inspirer is produced, and since its production is a trade secret, there are always many Guild of Spears guards patrolling the area.
426551214:
Asre

Asre is a town in Almhult. It is located on the southern coast of Cape Almhult on the shores of the Steaming Sea, between Surtse to the west and Roshik to the east.
426609104:
St. Caspieran's Salvation

3 locations:
  • Gas Forges
  • Lissalan safe house
  • Zincher's Arena
433452034:
Ropecliffs

Ropecliffs is a neighborhood where smugglers from the Precipice Quarter gather, having taken up the trade to survive the calamity that befell the district. Through a system of pulleys attached to various abandoned buildings and the cliff itself, cargo can be hoisted from the sea to the city, though it's not very safe, especially in bad weather. Due to the risk, most of the jobs they take are very lucrative, and they've installed a collapse system in their bases to destroy any evidence of their involvement if they're caught.
433920894:
Leydis

Leydis is a moss-covered ruined city located in the wood north of Rookwarden in the River Kingdoms region known as Loric Fells. It was founded by a Taldan prospector who struck pay dirt nearby, but was abandoned soon after it was founded. Its marble buildings and water-stained mosaics, not to mention the wealth in the abandoned nearby mine, still attract creatures and adventurers, but no one has been able to claim it for more than a year. Those who enter the ruins often find the skeletons of their predecessors hanging from its walls. Leydis is currently held by the medusa Chryphaena and her blind zombies.
453541124:
Turning Leaf

The Turning Leaf is a very popular restaurant in Eastgate that focuses on fish and vegetable dishes sourced from the Green Ridge. For two or three days each month, traveling chefs make appearances, preparing specialties from far-flung regions around the world. Tickets for these days are not only expensive because of the food but also because they are a significant social status symbol in the city, and there is fierce competition to get even one seat at the table. The owner is a ghoran named Qidesca who doesn't understand the intricacies of comedy, but for some reason, their stand-up routines have the clientele roaring with laughter each time they perform.
455801014:
Kazuhn City

Kazuhn City serves as the capital of the Kazuhn Prefecture in the heart of Taldor, but all is not well in Kazuhn City. Almost three quarters of the city stands empty: half having been abandoned, and another quarter ravaged by the great fire of 4702 AR. Most of what remains are industrial buildings devoted to keeping the agricultural produce of Kazuhn Prefecture endlessly flowing to the distant capital of Oppara.

The regions of Kazuhn City abandoned by polite society, now known as Squatter City, are far from truly empty. A shadow economy now nestles amongst the ruins of Kazuhn. This economy revolves around drugs and other illegal produce, particularly flayleaf, and even the best efforts of the city guard, led by Almoril Tersian, have failed to root out this corruption. The nobility often talk of finally demolishing the abandoned regions of the city but the expense has thus far prevented them from doing this. So, Squatter City remains a home to criminals, the destitute, and the monsters and vermin that prey on both.

The Brotherhood of Silence moved to this location in 4725 AR and have begun to influence the region; the Lion Blades are monitoring their activities.
458729864:
Matten Cleave

Matten Cleave is a town in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos used primarily by local farmers for transporting crops to the port of Kerrick and buying agricultural supplies. It is located near the northwestern shore of the Isle of Kortos on the Inner Sea, northeast of Kerrick.
459839594:
Anactoria

Anactoria is a settlement in the rural inland of the Ustalavic county of Caliphas on the banks of the Droa River and south of the Albria Woods.

The village is famous across Ustalav for the Maid of Anactoria, a corpse that was found in the local bogs in an incredibly well-preserved state despite its great age, which is now displayed in one of the village's cottages. The locals make their living cutting peat, which is exported across Ustalav as a cheap fuel.
462813164:
Totra

Totra, founded in -2587 AR by the Conquering Pharaoh An-Hepsu II, is the largest of Osirion's ports outside Sothis. The city is often seen not only as a symbol of the country's power, but a symbol of its excesses as well. When the An Dynasty subjugated what would become Thuvia and Rahadoum with its fleets (known as the Great Atoqua), Totra saw an influx of slaves who would build the country's pyramids and monuments. The city's under-classes and nobility are still almost exclusively Garundi, and harbor suspicion and prejudice against Keleshites for their millennia of foreign rule. Following the death of the Fallen Pharaoh and the Qadiran invasion of 1532 AR, many of Totra's ancient monuments were toppled by the invaders. In a turnaround, the city's harbor now hosts a red granite statue of An-Hepsu II, built on a new island created from the toppled statues of Qadiran satraps.

The city is loyal to Khemet III, but the nobility bemoan the heavy taxation they suffer.
466941644:
Mechitar

Mechitar (pronounced meh-KEE-tar) is the capital of the undead-ruled realm of Geb, and its second-largest city. Its architecture consists of Osirion-style pyramids, jet black in colour, each home to one of the Blood Lords or influential local families. The largest, the Cinerarium, serves as the palace of the nation's ruler, Geb, who conquered the city in -1108 AR to found his kingdom. Mechitar's busy harbour is the main outlet for the country's food exports. The city's largest temple is devoted to Urgathoa, and has both living and undead staff.
477669204:
Jerg's Repair Shop

Jerg's Repair Shop is a repair shop with a pier on the River Scorpius due to the high volume of traffic it receives daily. Retired adventurer Jerg Alshelner runs the shop, using his adventure funds and magic to repair items and vehicles brought to him.
478840174:
Miluvabrecht

Miluvabrecht is a ruined fortress in the Tanglebriar region that once served as a retreat for members of the magical order led by the elven wizard Aelthian. After he disappeared while fighting Treerazer, the place was abandoned and has been used as a lair by various demons and monsters for centuries.
487048824:
Kuratown

Kuratown is a small town in Numeria populated largely by orcs.
487492724:
Graybanks

Graybanks is an abandoned Nirmathi town south of Kraggodan.
500602284:
House of Seven Faces

The House of Seven Faces is a Westgate temple dedicated to Desna where her followers can rest. The clergy dislike ostentatious cathedrals, so there is little decoration except for the seven faces that appeared on the facade the first night the temple was built, and whose origin remains a mystery. There are many theories: some link the seven faces to Thassilon, others suggest they represent Sivanah and that Desna and she are the same person—a theory met with laughter or annoyance by the clergy. But the most sensible theory points to a connection with the Sevenfold Cynosure, where she dwells, the realm being a tribute to the seven planets Rovagug destroyed first. The faces, then, represent seven cultures from these planets that may have arisen and they also serve as a memorial.
503480744:
Windspire

Windspire is an independent roost of the Windspire tribe of strix in Devil's Perch, within the borders of the Chelaxian Archduchy of Hellcoast.
512131404:
Ygrath's Pier

Ygrath's Pier is one of the cyclopean monuments of ancient Thassilon, built with the labor of enslaved rune and stone giants. It is located in the western edge of the Inner Sea, which was the southwestern-most point of the Thassilonian nation of Eurythnia.
515137964:
Absalom Chirurgeon Dispatch

The Absalom Chirurgeon Dispatch is where the doctors, physicians, and healers of the Westgate district meet. It operates similarly to the practices of smaller towns where there is no hospital, and the doctor travels to the patient's home when called. The Sally Guard maintains magical communications with the Dispatch, allowing them to act quickly in emergencies. Although their primary focus is the district's health, they can be employed throughout Absalom and swear to assist in situations such as a siege. Classes are also offered on how to provide basic medical services, and they are always hiring new doctors, paying those with magical healing powers quite well.
541156454:
Bastion of Light

The Bastion of Light is a temple of Sarenrae located in the southwestern Gravelands, near Roslar's Coffer. As of 4722 AR it was fortified by the Burning Sun tribe orc Uirch and his band of Sarenite orcs. They work on both restoring the temple and defending it from any undead that come near.
553766874:
Deverin Villa

3 locations:
  • House of Welcome
  • Kaijitsu Townhouse
  • Matador's Lodge
557759794:
Hedgerow Holloway

Hedgerow Holloway is one of the most luxurious and comfortable inns in all of Greengold, where foreign dignitaries stay when they need lodging in the city. The surrounding gardens contain hedge mazes and statues of various animals, as well as beautiful fountains and benches where one can sit and relax.
585556884:
Gyanpad

Gyanpad is a mountain within the larger Lohaparbat Mountains of Vudra. It is most famous for the garuda monk Mahadev, who has meditated near its peak for over a decade. A small cult has developed around Mahadev to help keep his body nourished and alive, although they claim his spirit has already ascended to a higher plane and may be in the process of becoming a god. Mahadev's psychic emanations have also drawn monstrous yetis and wendigos to Gyanpad and the surrounding area.
597812074:
Saintsgrove

Saintsgrove is a small area of ancient olive trees in the south-east of the county of Sinaria, in Ustalav, lying near the southern banks of Lake Prophyria, and to the south-east of Karcau.

Legends speak of a priestess of Urgathoa who performed a sacrilegious ceremony to transform herself into a Daughter of Urgathoa at this spot, perverting a shrine of Desna and sacrificing the followers of the Song of the Spheres as part of her ritual. Such blasphemy apparently stirred Desna to direct action against the priestess by immediately ageing the olive trees by a thousand years, trapping the blasphemer and preventing her ritual from succeeding, while providing a grave for her dead worshippers.
599894944:
Grand Bazaar

Nearly anything can be found for sale in the Grand Bazaar of The Coins district in Absalom. The area has few permanent buildings, and most goods are sold from tents, carts, portable stages, and merchant wagons. Many merchants use dancers or illusionists to draw a sufficient crowd to make a sale. After dark, the Grand Bazaar becomes quite dangerous as criminals from the Docks and Puddles districts come here in search of heavy purses.
609900294:
Dusk Market

The Dusk Market is a wandering bazaar that sets up within the shells of fallen temples, theaters, and other opulent ruins within the Westcrown district of Rego Cader. Here the brave and foolish buy contraband, under the very eyes of bribed rundottari (local watch). Drugs, exotic poisons, strange beasts, and kidnapped slaves can all be found in this semi-secret marketplace.

Run by a secret syndicate, it takes place at a different ruin each day in the two hours before sundown—hence its name—to avoid Order of the Rack Hellknights and the city's dangerous creatures. For a price, almost any local ne'er-do-well can tell visitors where the market will set up next.
611394764:
Orisini Academy

2 locations:
  • Endrin Military Academy
  • Old City Hall
614645444:
Qatada Nessudidia

The Qatada Nessudidia is the largest temple to Asmodeus in Westcrown, and one of the largest buildings east of the Arodennama. Located in Rego Sacero, it stretches four stories tall and is capped with a red crystal skylight roof over its altar that glows with such sinister light that it is difficult to miss at night.
625357864:
Benedict's Hope

Benedict's Hope is a small, rocky island in the Andoshen River just outside the harbor of the Andoren capital of Almas. A small lighthouse, whose ever-burning flame guides ships into port, stands on the island. At the lighthouse's feet sits a small, brass statue of Alysande Benedict, shown hunched over a desk and scribbling with a feather stylus.
634130674:
Shogokabe

Shogokabe is the capital of Hofunawan Province in Minkai and the nation's primary fishing port. Being so closely involved with water, its docks are lined with so many shrines that no resident would know all of the deities' names, but they would revere each one all the same.
636449024:
Ulduvai

Ulduvai was the last of the Shory flying cities in Garund and the Inner Sea region. In a misguided effort to restore the Shory Empire, the sorcerers of Ulduvai consorted with the powers of the Dark Tapestry, which ended up with the entire population being driven mad by a shoggoth servant of Azathoth. The city crashed upon Mirrshock Glacier in the southeastern Mwangi Expanse, south of Mount Dowama in the Shattered Range in -507 AR.

Three ancient Shory sorcerers survived to the present, albeit as undead creatures: Yakmar, the insane adventurer-mage; Yithdul, the arcane diplomat; and Banderak, former head of the city's aeromantic corps.
647688354:
Arsmeril

The cliff-side elven village of Arsmeril can be found where the northwestern Mierani Forest meets the Steaming Sea. Along with the settlement of Crying Leaf, it is the only elven settlement in Varisia, and like its companion on the far side of the Mierani, it is an outpost of the elven nation of Kyonin who wish to keep a closer eye on the ruins of Celwynvian.
660075864:
Temple of the Shining Star

1 locations:
  • Pitview Pub
665563164:
Mortecant

Mortecant is a neighborhood in the southern part of the Precipice Quarter where followers of Urgathoa, seeking to be corrupted by the area's necromantic energies, move from abandoned house to abandoned house, weaving through a tangled web of people as if they were undead. Even the knights of Vigil's Hope have killed several mobs without realizing they weren't truly undead. Not all members of this community are inherently evil; some cannot move because the neighborhood's reputation would lead to their new neighbors killing them if they discovered their origins. Thus, they are trapped between the Knights of Lastwall and the wall of semi-dead mobs at their backs.
665647744:
Academy of the Reclamation

The Academy of the Reclamation in Nerosyan is an academy focused on the preservation and restoration of knowledge and history from the lost nation of Sarkoris and its diaspora following the closure of the Worldwound into the Sarkoris Scar. This extends to pursuing secrets of Sarkorian gods and the Sarkoris Scar itself.
673794544:
Seal of Kazutal

The Seal of Kazutal is a simple stone monument that serves as a shrine to the goddess in Absalom. Created by visitors from Arcadia while passing through, the monument has become a rallying point for freed slaves who are inspired by Kazutal's anti-slavery stance. Although the visitors from Arcadia are no longer there, the shrine is cared for by those who love freedom and their community.
677467174:
Prada Hanam

1 locations:
  • Pure Temple of the Maharajah
680801094:
Lantern Lodge

After being purchased by Venture-Captain Amara Li for a large sum of money, the Lantern Lodge in the Petal District of Absalom reflects the finest culture that the city has to offer. During her many trips to Absalom, she purchased many furnishings for the lodge and has brought them back to Goka, currently on display even today. The lodge is kept extremely clean and Amara Li expects her guests to conduct themselves professionally. Any Pathfinder who is heading on a long journey usually stops by this lodge before leaving.
683018104:
Shrine of Convocations

The Shrine of Convocations serves as the meeting place for the Umbral Court, where they resolve disputes, receive reports, and determine policy for the coming months. This convocation takes place during the fall and spring, where members debate and decide on the best resolutions, which are then decided democratically. The Black Triune only intervenes in emergency situations, where its orders are unquestionable and absolute.

If a member of the Umbral Court fails to attend the convocation, they are punished with death, and on occasion, various rivals have caused their opponents to be absent from the event to eliminate them.
683391124:
El-Fatar

A Pathfinder discovered an enormous set of catacombs beneath a ruined city of Ancient Osirion, in northern Katapesh. Adventurers came to the ruined city to explore the ancient tunnels and over time the small town of El-Fatar sprang up amidst the ruins. The intriguing artifacts discovered attracted many researchers and historians as well.
683615104:
Iron Mountain

The Iron Mountain is located in the Wall of Heaven range of Tian Xia south of Quain. A platform guarded by sentinel statues of foo lions was built long ago near one of the peaks of the Iron Mountain. This platform contains twin gates: one leading to Heaven, and the other to Hell. Once every 12 years, the imperial dragon known in Quain as the Celestial Dragon visits the site and grants one wish to a royal maiden.
691128354:
Piren's Bluff

Piren's Bluff is a small border hamlet in Andoran that sits atop a flat rock overlooking a narrow canyon leading deep into the Aspodell Mountains. It is surrounded by a tall wooden palisade with six watchtowers. An ancient keep watches over the hamlet from the highest elevation and protects Andoran's western border. The keep is surrounded by massive stone walls and guard towers, and has always served as home to the leaders of Piren's Bluff.
693230894:
Dead Villages

The Dead Villages are a collection of abandoned villages in the Brazen Frontier section of southern Osirion. They were once inhabited by Pharasmin death cults who believed that one's afterlife could be positively influenced by choosing the time of one's demise. This led the villagers to engage in ritual combat to the death as well as suicide. These practices guaranteed that the cult soon ran out of members, leaving the villages empty.

As of 4714 AR bones could still be found in the central squares of these empty settlements, and distressed undead such as allips haunt them at night. Although the cultists believed they were worshiping Pharasma, its leaders secretly were devotees of Sifkesh, demon lord of heresy and suicide.
696797094:
Irorium

The Irorium is an enormous open-air coliseum located in Absalom's Foreign Quarter that showcases non-lethal gladiatorial combat, mock sea-battles, religious festivals, and many other types of events and is the largest arena in the Inner Sea region.
702258354:
Hesperix Manor

Hesperix Manor is an estate on the border of the Umbral Basin and was the residence of the Chelaxian diplomat Perevill Hesperix, but it fell into disuse after his death during the Chelaxian Civil War. Architecturally, the manor harks back to the Chelaxian styles before the Thrune Ascendancy: geometrically symmetric and grey. A few decades ago, Celefin of Pangolais, an agent of the Umbral Court, purchased the manor and it has served as her home ever since.

A follower of the Belevais Doctrine, which precludes undead from following Zon-Kuthon's faith, Celefin of Pangolais retired from government service after realizing she could not advance. She has spent her retirement writing manuscripts on how to eliminate the undead, which has put her on the radar of certain members of the Umbral Court who would like to see her punished for her insolence.
709415874:
Seven Stelae

The Seven Stelae is a collection of massive pillars located between Shiman-Sekh and the port city of Totra in Osirion. The red granite pillars bear the symbols of Pharaoh An-Hepsu I. Several doors and chambers in the pillars have been found.
718978074:
Wartle

Though it is not a new settlement, the town of Wartle remains something of a frontier trading post, primarily populated by swampers and trappers who do not mind the inhospitable environment amidst the Mushfens. The buildings of the town rise out of the murky swamp water on stilts, connected by wooden boardwalks. Major exports of the town are peat, fungus, and furs, mostly sent down the Yondabakari River to Magnimar. The town sports a few successful brothels and a precariously tilted tavern called the Lean-To, which serves a local liquor called Bog Grog.
722741864:
Highhelm

8 locations:
  • Ashen Bulwark
  • Caldamin
  • Kovlar
  • Lyperro
  • Mount Kla
  • Ringsmith Ranch
  • Rolgrimmdur
  • Vault of the Forgotten Prophet
727897414:
Turpin Rowe

Turpin Rowe is a small logging town on the Isle of Kortos managed by the Yalbrough Consortium of Absalom. Its economy is driven by the lumber trade but derives its name from the nearby distillation of turp spirits.
730138974:
The Ramps

The Ramps are a series of timber and stone blocks that connect the Inner City and the Lower City. They start at the gates and are designed to collapse in the event of a siege. The ramps are 50 feet wide, and both sides have safety rails to prevent falls, although three or four people die each year from falls here; whether these are accidents or something else is unknown. Various illicit activities take place in the shadow of the ramps, and many criminals take refuge here in search of new victims to rob.
743103794:
Gardens of Shepeska

The Gardens of Shepeska are located on the northeastern tip of Osirion, where the Inner Sea meets the Obari Ocean. They are tended by druids who have granted many saplings to the crown prince at Sothis.
747628064:
Winter Grove

The Winter Grove is an ancient druid's grove that lies within the Barrowood in Cheliax. Ruined and blackened, the grove predates the old empire of Taldor, before the rise of Cheliax as an independent nation.

Rumours say that within the grove lies a summoning circle of ancient origin, and that within this circle Abrogail I made her dark dealings with Asmodeus. In recent times, locals to the region have witnessed members of the royal family making regular (but secretive) visits to the site, always bringing sacrificial victims with them.
750491264:
Gorumarrux

The Gorumarrux is a temple of Gorum and combat venue in Absalom's Ascendant Court. Its arena master and high priest of Gorum is Jaruke Dalagander, and as of 4720 AR Ledford was its undefeated champion.

The fate of the Gorumarrux after the death of Gorum in 4724 AR is unknown.
753925434:
Norboro Ashes

Norboro was the second town built on the cursed island of Whyrlis Rock, in the Shackles. It was founded only 6 months after its sister town of Heskott Bay. At about the same time that the residents of Heskott Bay vanished into thin air, the town of Norboro turned to ash. The location is now known as the Norboro Ashes.
801433614:
Kirahata

Kirahata (pronounced kee-RAH-hah-tah) is a hedonistic city located in southeastern Chu Ye that is popular with tourists. Talented humans travel to Kirahata to purse stardom, though mistakes in the oni-ruled land can become deadly. Kirahata's governor is Tamami, an oni with the wealth to challenge Chu Ye's ruler Tsuneni but prefers the novelty of meeting her city's newcomers over the responsibilities of rulership.
816790634:
Hunthul

Hunthul is the capital of the relatively new hobgoblin nation of Oprak. This is where the largest connection to the Onyx Citadel in Oprak is located.
826460534:
Feasting Hall of Cayden Cailean

In Mivon of the River Kingdoms, an unusual hill devoid of vegetation has a ring of standing stones on its summit. At irregular intervals these stones transform into a feasting hall from which comes music and laughter, and those who enter on these occasions can join in the revelry. Some say that this is the Feasting Hall of Cayden Cailean, the god of ale and wine.

In the morning, the standing stones replace the hall once more, although the hillside reeks of ale and wine. As of 4710 AR, the last occurrence was also marked by the presence of a large quantity of blood among the stones.
837282704:
Maw of Karth

The Maw of Karth is a cyclopean ruin that dates back to the era of the Koloran Empire. Built in Iobaria's Icerime Peaks, the Maw of Karth is a massive fanged skull carved into the mountains, towering over two neighbouring peaks to create a gate, with Karth's tongue as stairs and fangs as siderails that connect a massive stone doorway to the Koloran Roads. Many monsters see Karth as a god, and creatures such as goblins and kobolds favour the site as a location for ambushes.
848050194:
Wreck of the Jenivere

The Jenivere was a Wyvern-class Sargavan merchant vessel that plied the waters of the Arcadian Ocean, making the 100 day, 3,400 mile-long run between its home port of Eleder and the Varisian city of Magnimar. Resupply and trading stops along the way included the cities of Senghor, Bloodcove (Mwangi Expanse), Port Peril, Quent, Ollo (The Shackles), Ilizmagorti (Mediogalti Island), Corentyn, Pezzack, Kintargo (Cheliax), and Magnimar.
853970354:
Threshold

Threshold was a prison tower in the High Cairns of Sarkoris reserved for those who practiced arcane magic. It became the epicenter of the Worldwound upon its opening in 4606 AR.
865899174:
Ziplatna

Ziplatna is the northernmost of the cities known as the Follower's Rests and acts as hub for trade between Xopatl and the neighbouring kingdoms of Innazpa and Razatlan. Ziplatna is renowned for its hunger for labour and anyone in need of work can visit the city and find it on one of the ships leaving Ziplatna's docks.
873357914:
Gloomspires

The Gloomspires are a collection of rocky spires that rise from the Arcadian Ocean. They are located due south of Mediogalti Island, north-northwest of the Shackles, and southwest of the Eye of Abendego.
889060834:
Falcon's Hollow

2 locations:
  • Elara's Halfway House
  • Lumber Consortium Camp
898785944:
Blackraven Hall

Blackraven Hall is a fortress constructed for the sole purpose of monitoring the eastern border of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings from the monstrous creatures in neighbouring Irrisen. It is the largest fortress in the nation, and overlooks the Rimeflow River in the eastern Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach.
910381354:
Beldrin's Tower

8 locations:
  • Boneglutton Pit
  • Darkgate
  • Gulgamodh
  • Mortecant
  • Ropecliffs
  • Spiral Shrine
  • Vigil's Hope
  • Wracked Rock
923413684:
Doommark

Doommark is the seat of Molthune's Umbral Basin province and home to the only significant fetchling population in the nation, many of whom work for the territorial governor, General Lord Francyne Magga.
929439414:
Purt

Purt is a small settlement located along the Eldress River in Nirmathas. Protected on all sides by a wall of thorns 20 feet high and eight feet thick, the hamlet's human farmers grow the largest cabbages in the region. Purt's citizens know the several hidden passages through the wall of thorns. The druids of Acorn's Rest helped build the wall to protect the inhabitants from Molthuni mercenaries.
947222774:
Beehive

Deep within the Barrier Wall mountains of Katapesh, a network of chambers joined by winding tunnels forms a well-protected complex. Known as the Beehive, it is located on the southern road to Nex and has a number of widely known entrances.

Inhabitants have included smugglers, slavers, bandits, kholo, dwarves, and bee-like thriae, from which the tunnels got their name. The dwarves moved out when nothing valuable was found behind the rock walls. Currently, a hive of formians inhabits the Beehive, though they mostly toil digging new tunnels underground and rarely interact with the surface.
962095604:
Bank of Absalom

The Bank of Absalom has a far-reaching power, influencing trade and finance beyond the City at the Centre of the World across the Inner Sea region. From the Bank of Absalom, its bankers can influence the flow of trade and commerce by adjusting interest rates, approving (or not) massive loans, and issuing debt collection orders. The Bank of Absalom is nominally a secular institution, rather than a temple devoted to Abadar, the god of trade, but, in reality, the Bank of Absalom is run by Vroclaw of Brevoy, the most powerful Archbanker of Abadar in Absalom. The Bank of Absalom takes its religious orders from the Vault of Abadar in the Ivy District, but Vroclaw controls all loans and debt collection. Vroclaw's critics accuse him of facilitating the exploitation of indigenous peoples to extract profit, a claim Vroclaw does not deign to refute.
965056204:
Hatavit

The settlement of Hatavit was a small lumber camp located in Qadira, north of the Southern Tapur Forest. Designed to house slaves who harvest the bounty of the nearby forest, the town was also a post for woodworkers and carvers.

As of 4717 AR, and as early as 4709 AR, various accounts suggested that Hatavit had recently been hit by an unseasonable sandstorm. This storm either destroyed its watchtowers, allowing its slaves to escape into the forest, or it had destroyed the camp altogether and forced all of its inhabitants into the northern woods. Rumors also suggested that the sandstorm was a magical event designed to free the camp's slaves. In any telling, the camp's inhabitants have not been heard from since.
972787024:
Harborhorn

The Harborhorn is the home of Port Peril's harbormaster, who is responsible for inspecting every ship that docks at the pirate port. It is located on its own small island just off the larger island of Crescent Harbor.

As of 4712 AR, Harborhorn was the home of the notoriously stern dwarven harbormaster Tsojmin Kreidoros. Little goes on within the bustling city that escapes the harbormaster's notice.
1003718124:
Wisher's Well

3 locations:
  • Habe's Sanatorium
  • Paddlefoot Farm
  • The Pit
1004201654:
Nicyruse

Nicyruse was an ancient city of the Jistka Imperium located today in southern Avistan, on the Inner Sea coast. It housed one of the largest temple-forges in all the Imperium, rivaling even that of Rachikan. When Jistka's doom became imminent, divine earthquakes tore the region apart and city's forges were abandoned. Now the seaside ruins of Nicyruse have mostly eroded away by the Inner Sea.

In 4712 AR, reports from the Chelaxian city of Hinji to the east stated that a recent storm had cleared a new entrance to the buried site. Fisherfolk said that mechanical creatures began moving across the shore near the ruins, grabbing fish from the ocean with their bare hands, and taking them back into the ruins. The Pathfinder Society has called for adventurers to explore the ruins and bring back any information about lost Jistka.
1005880004:
Vynoren Estate

The Vynoren Estate is one of the oldest and most magnificent buildings in Greengold, and is the residence of the aristocratic Vynoren family. Because they offer their home as accommodation for visiting dignitaries, it is always maintained by a cleaning staff who keep everything in perfect condition.
1006532334:
Halmyris

Halmyris is a settlement in the Halikarnassos Hills of southern Cheliax, just north of the Cliffs of Fury and located at the eastern end of the Archduchy of Longmarch.
1009612574:
Gozarin Necropolis

The Gozarin Necropolis is a vast field of tombs, crypts, and graveyards within the Tremor Valley in central Osirion, west of the Pillars of the Sun.
1019430234:
Grand Dance Hall of Kortos

The Grand Dance Hall of Kortos is an ornate, round theater on the district of The Coins in the city of Absalom.

The city's richest merchants and most ambitious nobles gather in the theater to organise deals in order to control the economy of the Inner Sea. Admission is free to those in the appropriate attire, but access to the hall's three balconies requires increasingly audacious fees as the guest ascends the levels.

The entertainment includes the finest Vudrani dancers and gymnasts from throughout the Inner Sea and beyond.
1036740354:
Wreck of the Nightvoice

1 locations:
  • Fungus God
1038287364:
Wilkesmont

Wilkesmont is a small town located inside the Shroudwood in the River Kingdom of Daggermark. The town was the home of the bandit lord Miklos Jakab, self-proclaimed ruler of the Shroudwood.

Jakab, the last serious bandit threat to Daggermark, was killed in 4566 AR; his followers swore fealty to Daggermark. Due to its location in the wood, the town is relatively cut off from the rest of the kingdom and an easy target whenever the Daggermark military fails to protect it. Wilkesmont has been burned to the ground three times, and each time has been rebuilt bigger than before. The town lacks any fortifications, relying on the heavy wood to keep most raiders away.

Recently, a bandit lord from Sevenarches boasted he will claim the Shroudwood, so the residents of Wilkesmont plan to use assassins from Daggermark to stop him.
1049270364:
Kerrick

12 locations:
  • Castinlee
  • Matten Cleave
  • Cawshax
  • Arbo
  • Stump
  • Ferny
  • Elyon
  • Wellspring Tower
  • Liferoot Stone
  • and 3 others…
1055324354:
Marsh Tower

The Marsh Tower, also known by locals as the Marsh Stone Tower or the "Tower of Empty Stones", is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower is a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat 10-foot-wide top and four uneven tiers.

The tower is located on the northern side of the Isle of Kortos, near the small town of Willowside. Its aeon orb was stolen by the Circle of Stones in 4710 AR, and the tower was subsequently inhabited by a splinter group of the Circle's nature priests known as the Empty Stones. The loss of the aeon orb accelerated the advance of the Welt—a blight that as of 4720 AR threatened to overtake the tower despite the efforts of the Empty Stones.
1055475474:
Al'Hakam Estate

The Al'Hakam Estate is an impressive manor house on the edge of The Coins, near the Ascendant Court. Its grandeur is understandable, as it is not only the home of the influential merchant Aaqir al'Hakam but also of Emir Thalzar Gaatan, ambassador of Qadira. The couple lives there with their three adopted daughters, whom they adore.
1061402974:
Shepherd's Rock

The cliff-top citadel of Shepherd's Rock is situated in the Napsune Mountains in Rahadoum and has a commanding view of the nearby desert. As of 4711 AR, it was the centre of operations for the Pure Legion and residence of the organisation's leader Karsakim, the Sword of Man.

The citadel's Vault of Lies houses the Legion's vast store of confiscated religious writings. It also trains new recruits into the Pure Legion.
1066206014:
Nexian Embassy

The Nexian Embassy in the city of Katapesh is located in the Inner City district. Katapesh and Nex are natural allies, sharing similar views and business models. The embassy has been built after the dominant architectural style in the city of Quantium. A number of domed buildings topped by narrow spires surround a central courtyard where shrines to the gods form a central ring. Nexian flags cap the spires. The Nexian ambassador in Katapesh is Narlaguut Haraxis. He and his slaves and advisors reside in the embassy. The ambassador spends much of his time in court at the walled Palace of the Pactmasters maintaining goodwill between his country and Katapesh. He spends most of his spare time in Zandrek's Pesh Palace and the Golden Scarab.
1092415044:
Ghataking

Located in the central Napsune Mountains of Rahadoum, the mountain Ghataking is most famous for the foul inhabitants who dwell upon it. Ghataking is the lair of a zahhak, a powerful type of azi dragon, named Khanjar-Khanayr but more commonly known as "Stone Maw". He lairs on this peak awaiting a divine prophecy delivered by foul Ahriman himself and is served by a cult of Usij, evil div-worshippers all but wiped out by the church of Sarenrae.
1098712344:
Dhucharg

Dhucharg is the capital of the hobgoblin nation of Kaoling in northwestern Tian Xia, and is by far the most impressive of all the country's cities.
1108777424:
Kelmarane

Kelmarane is a ruined battle market village in northwestern Katapesh. The village was one of several in the Uwaga Highlands of the Brazen Peaks situated around the battle market. In 4689 AR the Pactmasters abandoned it to ruin. By 4707 AR the Kulldis tribe of kholo had claimed Kelmarane as its own. The village has since become a haven of kholo slavers, human bandits, and disreputable brigands from the northlands.

Aside from the ancient battle market and the ruined church of Sarenrae, most of the structures in Kelmarane are abandoned ruins. Most lack roofs or sturdy walls, but others are solid enough to provide temporary shelter.

The Pactmasters hired Almah Roveshki to reclaim the village.
1109525254:
District Courthouse

The District Courthouse of the Ascendant Court is where the district's less serious criminal trials are held, overseen by a trio of elderly judges of Abadar. Their judgments are fair, but the work here is usually quite slow, with a waiting list for filing a complaint or processing a grievance.
1113281174:
Fort Ristin

Fort Ristin marks the easternmost edge of the Chernasardo region. It is one of the oldest structures in the Southern Fangwood and is made of weathered blocks of smooth, gray stone. Chernasardo Rangers have occupied the fort for decades. It provides an excellent staging area against Molthuni forces invading Nirmathas from the south. Its location makes it an ideal base for border patrols. The fort's four stout towers, large keep, and sturdy walls provide a formidable defense. Its architecture is distinctly Chelaxian.

Fort Ristin was constructed by a regional governor named Abdel Ristin during the Everwar.
1115957784:
Wreck of the Brine Demon

3 locations:
  • Bloody Doll
  • Scallywag
  • Windwar
1129629414:
Steyr

Steyr is an inland town in far-eastern Andoran on the Carpenden Plains, approximately 150 miles west of the Sellen River. It houses a large community of expatriates from the tumultuous nation of Galt, many of them artists and philosophers.
1151515814:
Gauntlight Keep

The ruins of Gauntlight Keep, located on the edge of the Fogfen swamp near Otari, include its namesake lighthouse. Unbeknownst to the nearby town's residents, the keep's dungeons extend deep below ground.
1153280154:
Horun Plain

The Horun Plain is the central growing region of the anarchic nation of Galt, located in the west of the nation between the Fog Peaks and the Southern Hymbrian Forest. Before the Red Revolution, farmers here sent their grain to be milled in the city of Litran, but much of this commerce, like everything else, has been disrupted by Galt's ongoing troubles.
1172102184:
Blue Tower

Korvosa

Taggoret

1179795514:
Citadel Gheradesca

Citadel Gheradesca is the headquarters of the feared Hellknight Order of the Chain. Located near the Chelaxian city of Corentyn on a small spit of land connected to the mainland only at low tide, the citadel is thought to be Avistan's most secure prison. The Order of the Chain gladly holds any prisoners rightfully sentenced to jail by a government.

It can be accessed only during low tide, as there are jagged rocks and supposedly sea monsters that feed on the prison's dead. Potential invaders must climb wet, sharp stone stairs that wind up the outside of the island and overlook cliffs of the same jagged rocks that are under the waters. This is the only path up the island and is riddled with locations perfect for ambushes or hidden guard posts.

The prison part of the citadel is said to delve not only into the earth but also under the waves of the ocean surrounding the island. The lowest levels have special cells for more powerful prisoners that have been developed over many decades, and might house powerful creatures that must be caged as well as imprisoned. Some have unusual layouts, such as aquatic terrariums or enchanted game boards. Each is designed to house a specific type of prisoner.
1201820014:
Black Forks

Black Forks is an abandoned ancient monastery in the southern Verduran Forest. It is built around a strange, deep pool of black water, features strangely bestial architecture and sculpture, and is named for the two-headed forks once used in rites of self-torture by its long-deceased order.
1201850064:
Szamrak's Haven

Szamrak's Haven is a hidden enclave of androids, and the largest settlement of androids, in Numeria.
1213827234:
College of Mysteries

The College of Mysteries (formerly known as the House of Secrets) is the oldest school of magic in Absalom and is located in the Petal District. The college has a superb reputation in the city and is favored by a number of organisations, such the Javelin Gallery, whom they aid by enchanting weapons.
1226570974:
Hurlandrun

Hurlandrun was a Nirmathi village located near the source of the Deepcut River in the Mindspin Mountains in the south-west of the country. The disaster that led to the village's destruction was not due to war, but to the actions of individuals. A powerful mage named Mahalagris lived a quiet life in the village until he was killed and then returned as a dangerous undead being. This, combined with an attempted robbery of his tomb, unleashed his wrath, and the destruction he unleashed razed the village to the ground.
1230021984:
Okormirr

Okormirr is a now largely abandoned settlement in the northwestern corner of Iobaria. The first human settlement in Iobaria, it was founded in 752 AR when 20 survivors of an Ulfen expedition set up a longhouse to survive the winter. A settlement soon grew up around the longhouse, becoming Okormirr: the first of nine Ulfen settlements of Njalgard. Resettled many times over the centuries, Okormirr retains much of its original Ulfen character, with log lodges, dragon poles, and wooden palisades.

Okormirr sits on the northeastern shore of the Lake of Mists and Veils, south of the Ardshrod River. Now inhabited mostly by druids and their families, the city resents Prince Tzakiv Korya of Orlov's many promises to restore his city and kingdom to greatness. Okormirr is ruled by the Druid-King Aalgin, who requires all who live in Okormirr to swear allegiance to his druid circle and their god, Perbov, and their philosophy called the True Wyrd.

Regardless of these requirements, the results of Aalgin's rule has led to many positive changes in the area. These include the once-disgusting mire surrounding the town now being drinkable, and the town being protected from the elements by spruce canopies now interwoven to form a magical barrier.
1238415544:
Hyrantam

Hyrantam, the Drowned City, was once the capital of Lirgen, in what today is known as the Sodden Lands. The Saoc Brethren built hundreds of towers for use as observatories. The coming of the Eye of Abendego drowned the city, but the tops of the towers remain above the water. The city's remaining population live atop the towers, which they have connected with a crude network of rope bridges and pulleys. For some reason, aberrations from the depths of the Arcadian Ocean are drawn to these observatories, so the city is a dangerous place.
1241169294:
Beast

9 locations:
  • Besmara's Boardwalk
  • Devil's Own
  • Drownyard
  • Grog Pit
  • Lifter's Mooring
  • Meilin and Zhen Ships

  • Red Drake Warehouses
1248050954:
Snemmevik

Snemmevik is the northernmost town in Almhult, located at the head of a bay in the Gulf of Svallmundr. It serves as a handy base for prospectors seeking gold in the nearby Hellrung Mountains. The Maganis River empties into the gulf nearby.
1257859054:
Morta's Mortuary

Morta's Mortuary is the main funeral home in the Isgeri town of Breachill. The building is painted black and black curtains are draped in every window. Numerous paintings and decorations of psychopomps fill the building, as its owner, Morta Valaskin, has an affinity for such outsiders.
1266935964:
Kalsgard

The city of Kalsgard is located on the southern banks of the Rimeflow River in the northern reaches of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings known as the Thanelands. It is the largest city in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, and as such is the most cosmopolitan city for several hundred miles, housing communities of dwarves, gnomes, Varisians, and people from even further afield.
1276019334:
Neruma

Unusually for the Shackles, the city of Neruma is not on the coast. It is one of the few major settlements on the mainland, and is situated in the northeast along the Terwa River close to the borders with the Mwangi Expanse and the Sodden Lands. Neruma is a centre for trade between the Shackles and those two neighbouring regions. The main trading items purchased here are slaves, but other items of value also occasionally make their way along the river to Neruma.

The city's current ruler is Cousotta Shald. She is assisted by the Steel Cordage, a group of mercenaries supplied by the Shackles' ruling Pirate Council.

The city is built on lizardfolk breeding grounds (although the townsfolk are unaware of this), and is subject to regular attacks by lizardfolk seeking to reclaim their lost territory.
1281449164:
Administrative and Civic Center of Ravounel

26 locations:
  • Shadowsquare
  • House of Golden Veils
  • Songbird Hall
  • Humbright House
  • Hocum's Fantasmagorium

  • and 17 others…
1282222184:
Absalom Menagerie

The Absalom Menagerie is a park where a wide variety of animals live in climate-controlled enclosures behind magical walls designed to reduce the danger to visitors. Almost anyone can enter, as its founder bequeathed the park to the city with the stipulation that no member of the Blakros family be allowed entry, though the reason for this is unknown. The government pays the park's caretakers, who are usually members of the founder's family, a matter of tradition rather than obligation and Alina Muraabe is the High Warden of the park. The druids are divided between those who consider the place an affront to the natural order and those who believe that exposing urban dwellers to nature can foster greater appreciation and respect for it.
1284179234:
Otari

Otari is a small port town near the Immenwood on the south coast of the Isle of Kortos, about halfway between Absalom and Diobel. It is located at the head of a bay of the Inner Sea. It is a modern but relatively quiet settlement, and is popular among retired Pathfinders, many of whom operate businesses there. Important to the economy of Absalom as a whole is Otari's lumber trade: timber cut from the Immenwood arrives by road into Otari and, from there, is traded across the Inner Sea region.
1297408864:
Rachikan

The ruins of the ancient Jistkan city of Rachikan were discovered in 4709 AR by the Pathfinder Eldis Grone among the Pillars of Anferita in southwestern Cheliax.

It is the site of a Grand Athenaeum expedition led by Marianix Karn.
1302822714:
Braughleigh's Hollow

Braughleigh's Hollow is something of an open, yet monstrous, secret amongst the people of the Avin Prefecture for it is an ogre settlement. Nestled in the foothills of the Fog Peaks, far from any road or other settlements, this secret group of ogres is the remnants of a much larger population that once ravaged the Avin Prefecture. Now, however, they restrict their raiding to the foothills of the Fog Peaks. Braughleigh's Hollow serves as a meeting place for the other monstrous tribes of the Fog Peaks, many of whom engage in human slavery.

Rumour suggests nobles from Yanmass and even as far afield as the Northern Tandak Prefecture secretly visit Braughleigh's Hollow to acquire human chattel or quietly dispose of unwanted enemies or criminals. This foul trade is something of an open secret, but as long as the ogres stick to the mountains the Taldan authorities have proven willing to look the other way.
1312549354:
Copperwood

5 locations:
  • Crescent Manor
  • Kin Gate
  • Lantern Lodge
  • Silken Court
  • Spiralcross Cemetery
1315470434:
Rashiz

Rashiz is a Qadiran city that lies near the centre of the lush Maharev region. This region is one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira. Rashiz itself sits on the southern bank of the Meraz River, with the Maharev Jungle just to the north upon the opposite bank.
1322941894:
Valley of Shards

The Valley of Shards is a long shallow valley near the headwaters of the Asp River in Osirion. The valley's floor is full of shards of more than a thousand pots. The shards are inscribed with Ancient Osiriani hieroglyphs which seem to deal with magic, both divine and arcane. A small group of hermits study the shards, attempting to piece them together into a coherent message. The kholo roaming the region either have not noticed the hermits or have left them unharmed on purpose.
1325811054:
Blue Tower

The Blue Tower is the tallest building within the city walls of Absalom, and the third-tallest building in the whole city. Residents across the city use both the Blue Tower, the northwestern Spire of Nex, and the peaks of the Kortos Mounts as navigational landmarks.
1358009324:
Floater's Pond

Located near the vast salt flats of the Sabkha in central Katapesh, Floater's Pond is an oasis unlike any other, as it is a salt water oasis. The water seeps through the ground into the salt-laden sand, but thanks to the constant seeping water it never dries out. The water instead becomes inundated with salt.

Floater's Pond is so salty that it is almost impossible to sink in it, but that also means the pond is completely uninhabitable to aquatic plants or animals. Sometimes desperate pesh farmers come to Floater's Pond to harvest valuable nagri salt, but the process is time consuming because the farmer has to soak a sheet in the salty water and wait until it dries and leaves behind the valuable salt.
1362747174:
Viritash's Tail

Viritash's Tail is an unholy valley within the section of the Pardaparbat Mountains inside the Narhari Desert. It is named after the evil Vudran dragon deity Viritash, and a scepter of incredible potency made from Viritash's severed tail remains at the heart of the valleys. The land surrounding Viritash's Tail is filled with numerous linnorms and other corrupt dragons fighting to claim the scepter for themselves.
1366884294:
Lotheedar

5 locations:
  • Palace of Birdsong
  • Sotto
  • Stachys Pump House
  • Voinaris
  • Voinum Estate
1371739494:
Ekbet

The ruined city of Ekbet in Osirion is known as Gralgor-Ot by its sphinx residents. The ancient ruins are protected by the timeless guardians called living monoliths. These magically enhanced sentinels, powered by the sphinxes, protect the ancient ruins.
1389170504:
Tower of Twin Truths

The Tower of Twin Truths is the largest and most important temple of Nethys in Absalom.
1400935784:
Solanas

Solanas is a frontier town in the region of the River Kingdoms controlled by Daggermark.

As of 4710 AR it was ruled by the Solanas Concern criminal gang, and while the town's sheriff does what he can to keep the peace, the Concern interferes to protect its interests. Solanas' people are accustomed to daily killings and assassinations that happen every day, and the mayor had sent an emissary to Daggermark to beg lord Martro Livondar for help.

Solanas is home to an important Pathfinder lodge, complete with a main hall, a dormitory, and a stable full of fresh horses. The lodge is guarded by a group of mercenaries.
1428534404:
Castle Belverio

Castle Belverio is part of the northern wall of the Chelaxian city of Corentyn. The castle is partially ruined, although it has been rebuilt several times.

The Castle Belverio is one of the nine famous forts of Corentyn, according to the classical listing.
1435296494:
Yuming

Yuming is a massive city located in the former heartland of Imperial Lung Wa in the Successor State of Po Li. The nation is ruled by the powerful Oracular Council that allows no worship of other deities other than that of the idea of the Eternal Emperor.
1440210364:
Faded Hall of Lorngrim

The Faded Hall of Lorngrim was built by a long-forgotten dwarven lord of Zavaten Gura to serve as a summer palace. It is located in the Hills of Mournful Calling near the Avistani approach to the Crown of the World and overlooks a necropolis of dwarven tombs, barrows, and mausoleums.
1452371004:
Silver Star

The Silver Star, once a music store in the foreign quarter of the Jarvis End district of Kintargo, is as of 4724 AR a vacant lot.
1455662674:
Crystal Falls

The Crystal Falls is a 100-foot-tall waterfall in western Andoran, located in the southern Aspodell Mountains near the border with Cheliax. The beautiful site has the reputation of being haunted and is generally avoided by the local populace.

A secret Chelaxian base lies hidden inside one of the caves and the spies will silence any who learn of its existence.
1458504094:
Imnuxeos

Imnuxeos is an ancient, haunted remainder of the long-collapsed Ghol-Gan empire. Located south of the Terwa Uplands and north of Bloodcove, Imnuxeos is now little more than a stretch of isolated badlands dotted with remnants of colossal masonry from 10,000 years ago.

At the height of the Ghol-Gan civilisation, Imnuxeos was the most brutal fighting pit in the kingdom, a colossal coliseum where cyclops gladiators faced off against captured monsters, slaves, or even other cyclopes in bloodbaths of exceptional spectacle. The ghosts of these ancient gladiators still linger, and every night as darkness falls Imnuxeos's champions rise once more to face each other—and any mortal foolish enough to remain. Those who linger after sunset face undead cyclopes, horrific aberrations, and reanimated beasts of ancient bone.
1465253224:
Shoreline Road

Shoreline Road is a road that crosses Greengold and continues along the shore of Lake Encarthan to reach Druma in the southeast.
1475595854:
Corentyn
