1480318284:
Jangley's Shop

Jangley's Shop is a store run by the halfling brothers Pasker and Trill Jangley, who sell all sorts of curiosities. They use their extensive network of contacts to update their inventory daily, acquiring a wide variety of items at varying prices.
1482800074:
Rivenstone

Rivenstone is a submerged mountain within the Broken Bay region of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings that causes great trouble to passing ships. Rivenstone rises up from the depth of one of the deepest valleys of the Steaming Sea, splitting on its way up into seven separate peaks. The highest two peaks reach just below the sea's surface except at the lowest tides when they poke above the tideline; they are known to local sailors but seem to have a knack for sinking visiting ships. Another danger is the presence of a Dagon-worshipping tribe of intelligent gutaki devilfish. They are led by a cabal of a dozen priests, a mix of clerics and oracles, who use charmed giant octopuses and squid to bolster their tribe making them one of the deadliest threats to dwell within Broken Bay.
1497227694:
Atelier Kiritane

Atelier Kiritane is an accessories shop that creates designs using velstrac skin and bones, favored by the citizens of Pangolais. The shop's owner is Limris Kiritane, who designs the accessories. The clientele is divided between those who wear the accessories more casually and Kuthite fanatics who embed them in their skin, making them a part of their bodies.
1499068484:
Tiger's Eye Monastery

The Tiger's Eye Monastery is a remote building located in the northern forests of Jalmeray, far from local trade routes. It is said to be decorated with depictions of tiger-headed humanoids and currently is occupied by a group of bandits.
1505691994:
Ducotrion

Ducotrion is a single-story stone structure located in the center of the city to respond to any emergency. It also serves as the offices and barracks of the duxotar. Duxotar Ariende Jharnavar is in charge of the guard and is usually in the building.
1513023034:
Al-Varish

Al-Varish is one of the numerous massive trading cities that dot the Qadiran region of the Plains of Paresh, but is often overshadowed by the capital of Katheer. It is located at the head of the Jackal River, one of the two sources for the River Ladan.
1518617104:
Encarthan Coast

The Encarthan Coast is the stretch of coastline on the eastern side of Nirmathas that provides access to Lake Encarthan and includes the river deltas of both the Marideth River and the Tourondel River. Of all the nations bordering the lake, Nirmathas has the least-developed coastline, consisting of swamps where only fishing boats can navigate. Alder, gum, and willow trees cover the area, and the only large settlement in the region is Tamran, the capital. Fort Faelon is the second-largest, but is not much more than an inhabited ruin. Due to the war with Molthune and its maritime blockade, few exports pass through the area, leaving it isolated from other neighboring countries.
1518829694:
Wandering Monster

4 locations:
  • Bloom Cabaret
  • Chamber Grove
  • Dacilane Manor
  • Salhar Estate
1531084024:
Kiutu

Kiutu is a small village in the Mwangi Expanse near Terwa Lake. Most of the locals make a steady living through fishing, although most of the town's income is generated from building boats. Bonuwat tradition and Mbe'ke innovation have combined over the generations to create some of the regions most in-demand boats. Kiutu is also known for producing a surprising amount of artisans who become explorers and adventurers, though they remember to send money and other gifts back to their families.
1533566224:
Whitethrone

Home to the Jadwiga ("children of the witch-mother"), the descendants of the witch Baba Yaga, Whitethrone was a playground for evil nobles under the 100-year reign of previous Queen Elvanna's family. This has changed since the ascent of Elvanna's successor, Queen Anastasia, in 4713 AR.
1542840734:
Ukhrul

Ukhrul is one of the numerous prosperous cities of the Wide Water region of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. Ukhrul sits at the eastern end of the Seabound Road, which with its completion heralded a new era of prosperity for the once desolate region of the Western Ghats. Ukhrul itself, however, sits on the eastern side of the Johar Mountains in the north-west of the Wide Waters region, sited nearby the source of the Matra River.

Ukhrul has a reputation as a somewhat grim city, ruled by the militaristic Rajah Shavit, and is renowned for its production of firearms and other technological weapons. Rajah Shavit offers these weapons for export but only to distant mahajanapadas so that his expansionist ambitions cannot be hampered by the advanced weapons made in Ukhrul being used against him by his neighbours.
1554360074:
Fort Kragang

Fort Kragang is an occupied fort among the mountain realms of Zi Ha, originally constructed by invading hobgoblins from Kaoling under the leadership of Warlord Tsung-cha Kavangaki. It is currently occupied by a contingent of troops led by Ping-la Maningki, which harass local samsarans, raiding them for supplies and capturing them. Some samsarans of Zi Ha have hired mercenaries to oppose the hobgoblins, so it may only be a matter of time before a clash erupts.
1555918144:
Little Oppara

3 locations:
  • Broken Hook
  • Cayden's Arm
  • Pit of Raugsmauda
1562728614:
Kirya

The temple city of Kirya is located in the peaks above the Syrzemyan Highlands of Iobaria, and is accessible only by flight or via the cyclopean Koloran road that runs across the Grenalthrost and the Grenalf Plateau. An ancient cyclops temple city surrounding a clear mountain lake called Orost-Kar, the modern city of Kirya was founded by the Ulfen explorer Grenalf in 846 AR who named the city after his wife. Priests of various Iobarian faiths soon reconsecrated the temples to their own gods, and the city became a centre for pilgrimages from across Iobaria.

Kirya eventually became one of Iobaria's largest cities, with new building materials brought in from the Orostgard forest, until the logging awoke something, leading to the depopulation of Kirya by 1574 AR. Since then, various powers have taken control of Kirya, including a group of cyclopes and hill giants who built the Temples of Blood and Bone out of human bodies. The cyclopes and giants were cleared out by one of Iobaria's many plagues, and succeeded by hobgoblins, who ruled until the rise of New Iobaria in 3212 AR when humans reclaimed Kirya.

Under the leadership of grand druid Fedor Vasylk, the temple city is maintaining its independence in the conflicts between Orlov, Kridorn, and Mirnbay. Its inhabitants embrace 36 local nature gods who are seen as Erastil's children.

Dragons who were once allies of Iobaria's noble houses are known to inhabit the ruins beneath Kirya.
1564807574:
Courtesan and Unicorn

The Courtesan and Unicorn is a combination harrow parlor and brothel run by Vanyavi Czanji in the Lowcleft district of Magnimar. The harrow readings are often performed by non-Varisians dressed up as a caricature of true members of this ethnicity. Shoanti guards fanatically loyal to Czanji monitor the premises. Their pay consists of credit with the prostitutes.
1569773144:
Vespam Artisans

3 locations:
  • Feathered Fox
  • House of Truth and Clarity
  • Iudeimus Tenement
1572933844:
Vault of Silver and Ice

The Vault of Silver and Ice was a valuable mine in the Irriseni province of Bleakmarch, discovered during the reign of Queen Velikas.
1573909294:
Whitebridge Station

Whitebridge Station was an outpost of the Aspis Consortium on the Vanji River in the Mwangi Expanse. It was located roughly 200 miles upriver from the town of Bloodcove, and was a 200-foot-long fortified wooden bridge across the river with numerous wooden structures built upon it. The Consortium allowed others to use the bridge to cross the river in return for a small toll payment.

The Consortium was breeding the magical fungus known as arcophage here.
1574875574:
Cavern of the Sacred Shapes

The Cavern of the Sacred Shapes is a location in the Linnorm Kingdom of Icemark once used by the native Varki as a repository for their sculpture and other artwork. It was seized several generations ago by a tribe of adlets from the Stormspear Mountains that has defended it from outsiders ever since. Several attempts have been made by more adventurous Varki to retake the Cavern, but all have failed. Survivors of these attacks have reported that the adlets seem to hold certain of the cave paintings inside as sacred, leading to speculation that the adlets are actually ancient, cursed Varki who have returned to reclaim their birthright.
1576325174:
Eagle's Watch

The town of Eagle's Watch is located near the summit of Mount Veshka in Brevoy. It is the seat of House Orlovsky, one of the noble families of Brevoy and an ally of the vanished House Rogarvia.
1578496294:
Black Grave Tower

The Black Grave Tower is a pagoda located in the remote Wild Peaks region of the south-western reaches of the Kelsang Mountains in Zi Ha. The Black Grave Tower is devoted to Fumeiyoshi, god of envy and graves, and is made out of black basalt. It features Fumeiyoshi's three-eyed red visage on its front doors and its main hall includes an intricate tapestry featuring Fumeiyoshi fighting with the Prince of the Moon, Tsukiyo.
1586043404:
Bogsbridge

Bogsbridge is a small port on Motaku Isle in the Shackles. It is built in a marshy wetland completely unworthy of any development.

Falgood Boles, captain of the brig Bitter Wind, founded the small port after he had a vision of Besmara. He believed Besmara had tasked him to build a port in this marshy wetland. The devout pirate developed the settlement using his personal wealth, despite the boggy location and the fact that no large ship could use this harbor. Boles lived there alone with his wife for two years.

The marsh was gradually silting the harbor when Falgood Boles discovered a unique breed of snail in the surrounding marshes that produced a rare purple dye. The discovery made Falgood Boles a fortune and since then the town has grown. The town still continues to harvest the lucrative snails, transporting it via a sandy road to Lilywhite where the dye is shipped far and wide.

Boles's cheerful granddaughter, Tendry Boles, is the town's mayor. She also tends the inn her grandfather built, the thriving Besmara's Favor.
1589749084:
Ciricskree

Ciricskree—the Screeching Spire in the Strix language—is the largest strix settlement in the Inner Sea region and perhaps in all of Golarion. It is located in the southern Menador Mountains of Cheliax, in the Archduchy of Hellcoast.
1624712844:
Djefet

Djefet is a small town on the Sphinx River of Osirion that bears a terrible curse. At first glance, it looks like many other Osirian towns with reed-and-wood homes built on stone foundations, but this is where the similarities end. All of Djefet's residents bear a curse that causes their skin to turn ash-colored as they enter adulthood and then wrinkle and wither away. Residents who try to escape Djefet find that their paths inevitably always turn back to the town and are never able to escape. Legend tells of an artifact in the Pillars of the Sun that could put an end to the curse, but none of the residents have ever been able to leave to verify the validity of this claim.
1625200994:
Jaagiin

Jaagiin is a trade town in the Crown of the World that lies along the Path of Aganhei at the western end of Lake Buriyiim. The capital of the Osman Confederation, it is home to a permanent assembly of speakers who represent the numerous villages that crowd about the lake.
1629749414:
Mount Veshka

The summit of Mount Veshka is the tallest point in Brevoy, and the city of Eagle's Watch is located near its summit. It is the sole mountain in the Uvall Hills that span from the Golushkin Mountains in the west to the Icerime Peaks in the east.
1640743794:
Ushumgal

Ushumgal is a wealthy city on the Alavah Peninsula of Qadira.
1645562784:
Wandering Spheres

The Wandering Spheres is a massive ruined orrery located in central Kyonin with human-sized planetary spheres that once must have risen above the treetops. Its mechanism shattered long ago, and the many models of celestial objects now lie scattered about the glade, or are attached to metal beams that are twisted. No one knows how the orrery was destroyed, but what is stranger is that it models a solar system that is not that of Golarion.
1646094424:
Sphinx Head

The Sphinx Head or Black Sphinx is the largest sphinx statue in Osirion, located in the desert between Shiman-Sekh and the Junira River. It is constructed from black granite, and has sunk up to its chin in the desert sands. Its features are believed to be those of the Sky Pharaoh, Menedes I, staring into the center of Osirion.
1666220274:
Metro-Cathedral

The Metro-Cathedral is a church in Abadar, The Puddles, where the organ, which survived the earthquake of 4698 AR, is played every Oathday. The same cannot be said of one of its towers, which collapsed and crushed seven people. The cathedral is currently without a congregation, although a priest occasionally visits to inspect the structure.
1675195254:
Sangpo-Jong

Cut into the side of a mountain, Sangpo-Jong is the nominal capital of Zi Ha in Tian Xia and an isolationist city dominated by samsarans. Most outsiders are forbidden from the city, but for those who can enter, Sangpo-Jong is full of scenic wonders and epic stone carvings.
1677864384:
Tower of the Ironwood Watch

The Tower of the Ironwood Watch was a beautiful fortress of magical crystal erected by the elves of the Mierani Forest on the border with their neighbors in the kingdom of Cyrusian. The tower received guests of all kinds and was filled with luxuries for these diplomatic delegations. Ten millennia have left only the lower levels of the tower intact, and from the outside it appears as a curiously shaped hill in the treeless Velashu Uplands, as the forest receded until the area became grasslands. Drow of the House Vexidyre began to live in the structure after being driven from Celwynvian by the return of the elves, and Inexora Vexidyre, a follower of Shax, began raiding the surface for resources while they searched for a way to return home. In 4713 AR, a group of pathfinders discovered the location and defeated the drow cabal living within.
1680143334:
Palette Falls

The Palette Falls waterfall is where the green waters of Emerald Creek and the orange-hued Ironpass River merge before flowing into the Inkwater River. This area, located on the Nesmian Plains in Nirmathas, is the only region that mottled palette berries grow, which can be turned into a unique spice. The unusual water coloring has led to otters with speckled green fur. The pelts of these otters are sold at high prices in Canorate.
1681095524:
Tomb of the Bound Sphinx

1 locations:
  • Mephit Spring
1689064894:
Asleifar

Asleifar is a large whaling village on the coast of the Arcadian Ocean in the Thanelands of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.
1698085364:
Fey's End

Fey's End is a mysterious pit hundreds of feet deep. How it was formed and its exact nature are a mystery, but the effects it has on any nearby fey are clear. Fey creatures are drawn to the pit, and when they reemerge, they are dark, twisted versions of themselves.
1712395964:
Almas University

3 locations:
  • Avenue of the Gods
  • Cathedral of Aroden
  • Golden Cathedral
1720740814:
Golsifar

Golsifar is a small port town on Taldor's Inner Sea coast, located approximately 90 miles north of the mouth of the Jalrune River in the prefecture of Moda. It serves as the prefectural capital.

As the southern staging point for the Taldan Imperial Navy, the settlement unsurprisingly has strong military leanings.
1724601544:
Egregzia

The ruins of the Ghol-Gan city of Egregzia are located on the western side of the Terwa Lake in the Mwangi Expanse. The ruins are mostly buried beneath the alluvial grime that has spread from the lake in the thousands of years since Ghol-Gan's fall. Freshwater monsters lurk in the sunken structures of the dead cyclopes' waterside city, such as merrows, scrags, and ulat-kinis.
1746848524:
Silk Castle

The Silk Castle is a kite shop located in the Wise Quarter of Absalom. Its proprietor, Vittar Corusec, is often hired to judge special kite-fighting battles in the Irorium during the Gala of Sails and Kraken Carnival festivals held in spring and autumn respectively.
1748900624:
Felicity Vale

Felicity Vale is a valley in the Nesmian Plains in Nirmathas. In centuries past it was home to a Kellid town and a half-dozen smaller settlements, but all are now in ruin since a tyrant jelly took control of a hive of giant bees and began feeding on the human inhabitants. Swarms of mundane and giant bees have made the vale's natural ecosystem very productive with numerous fruit trees and flowers growing there. Despite its seemingly idyllic nature, many adventurers and lost wanderers who attempted to reap the vale's bounty roused the jelly's ire, were captured by giant bees, are became sealed in wax in their hive.
1768190134:
Al'Faran's Steel Restaurant

Doubtlessly one of Katapesh's strangest weapon shops, Al'Faran's Steel Restaurant is located in the Inner City area, but bears more resemblance to an eatery that a swordsmith's. Customers are taken to a table and presented with a selection of beverages and food, then given a list of the unique weapons that have been created by the master weaponsmith Diyab Al'Faran. He crafts bespoke weapons from the finest Drumish steel to give them near-perfect balance. As a result, his weapons cost substantially more than normal weapons.

While Al'Faran doesn't sell magical weapons, he does sell magical oils and alchemical substances that enhance weapons, such as silversheen or oil of keen edge.
1776188984:
Twingate

The city of Twingate is located in the north of the nation of Druma on the Gulf of Accord. It is a semipermanent city of tents where the curious come to be initiated into the mysteries of the Prophecies of Kalistrade.
1781462964:
Vische

The Ustalavic settlement of Vische is located in the county of Barstoi, close to its border with Numeria. The House of Solace is located in the town and there clergy of Pharasma are ordained.
1783080164:
Kintargo

1783285354:
Anthusis

Anthusis, formerly called Eleder, is the capital of Vidrian and the country's only deepwater port.
1789873424:
Heskott Bay

Heskott Bay was a town founded on the cursed island of Whyrlis Rock in the Shackles. It was located on one of the three decent anchorages left on the island. It was built as an outpost for the fleet of the pirate lord Baillie Heskott in the early years of the Shackles. It seems that three years after its foundation, all the residents of the town vanished into thin air. Those few who visited the abandoned town said that they heard the sounds of a busy city interrupted by screams of terror.

1815455744:
Cormegi Manor

Cormegi Manor was once the seat of Hest Cormegi, a wealthy plantation owner in the southwest of Sinaria county in Ustalav. When the cheap and morally bankrupt Cormegi began to kidnap the youth of Graidmere Swamp to work his fields, the people rose up against him. They were no match for Cormegi's thugs, however, and afterwards the corrupt landowner simply used their bodies to fertilize the soil. The children were finally freed, when their dead parents arose from their tilled graves and murdered Cormegi and his men. Having nowhere to run, the young people decided to occupy the manor home and continue farming it, still under the watchful eyes of their undead progenitors. The place is now staunchly avoided by the swampers of the region.
1817670044:
Moth and Flame

1 locations:
  • Hall of the Chronicles
1817969904:
Gap of Valballus

The Gap of Valballus is a wide, low mountain pass that connects the Hold of Belkzen to Ustalav to the east. It runs between the Tusk Mountains to the north and the Hungry Mountains to the south. Orc raiders traverse the Gap to sack the Ustalavic settlements in Canterwall. Varisian caravans and merchants also use the pass heading to or from Urgir.
1841733054:
Zlatomesto

Zlatomesto is a small town in Irriseni province of Wintercrux. It is located at the southern part of the province and serves as the shipping center for the ice diamonds mined at Hope Lost and the products of the mines and stone quarries of Skala Foothills. As of 4713 AR, Baroness Sabina Kseniya and her five sullen offspring ruled the town with iron efficiency. Zlatomesto's peasants worked hard, day and night under the lash like slaves, to load the caravans' wagons and sledges.

The Tin King Alley Tavern was the preferred place for the work gangs' masters. The tavern was famous for the potato vodka that it served, but its owner Liisu Nyetesky was a secret agent of the underground movement called the Heralds of Summer's Return, who valued the information she gathered from the drunken patrons concerning the schedule of the most important shipments headed east as it set up ambushes.
1849034634:
Growling Skald

Growling Skald is a mountain ridge of the larger Mindspin Mountains range that contains one of its few volcanically active areas. The low rumble churning underneath is a clear sign of geological activity, though the more poetically minded say that the rumble is a restless giant spirit that haunts the ridge. The ridge's elevation averages 11,000 feet.
1851207644:
Sanctum's Watch

Sanctum's Watch was built to guard a pass in the Southern Range of the World's Edge Mountains in the Taldan province of Ligos, a purpose which it no longer serves. Sanctum's Watch is a fortress expertly hidden amongst the mountains and the valley itself was a sight of much bloodshed in the early days of the Grand Campaign, when the Taldan army faced off against the forces of Qadira. Seen as an easy way to march an army into the soft belly of Taldor, the valley was eventually fortified, which discouraged Qadira from ever trying this approach again and led to disuse. After several centuries Sanctum's Watch was all but abandoned, with Taldan officials even forgetting how the hidden fortress was accessed.

1892933164:
Taggoret

The city of Taggoret was the center of the Everlasting Kingdom of Taggoret: one of the five dwarven kingdoms in the Five Kings Mountains, founded in 1562 AR by Taggrick I.

Nowadays Taggoret is a thriving city with iron gates and stone walls on the surface and refined great caverns within the mountain. It is located at the north end of the long trail of dwarven tunnels that run beneath the Five Kings Mountains. Its main industry is iron mining and smelting. Taggoret's iron is known as the finest in Druma and Andoran.
1894183024:
Ysborg

Ysborg is located just below the snowline on the northern slopes of Mount Gnarlfang, in the western Kodar Mountains at the far south-eastern corner of the Linnorm Kingdom of Southmoor, where borders provide no real barrier to the eternal winter of neighboring Irrisen.

Ysborg, the "Steading of the Ice Trolls", is something between a town and a fortress, consisting of crude wooden buildings and hide tents, all sized for giant occupants, surrounded by a stout wooden palisade. The entire fortress is surrounded by a moat of nearly freezing water inhabited by numerous river drakes.

Frost trolls, ettins, ogres, and other giants come from all across the Linnorm Kingdoms to join the army of the Blue Jarl, a huge powerfully muscled frost troll with a giant battleaxe curved from magical ice.

The streets of Ysborg are dangerous, with brutal fights and fresh blood on the snow to be a common sight. The Blue Jarl's "Winter Palace" is one of Ysborg's few stone buildings. It is nothing more than a blocky pile of icy rocks but it is well-fortified. The throne of the Blue Jarl is made from carved mammoth tusks and is decorated with human skulls. The Blue Jarl's kennels, just outside the palace are filled with winter wolves who have sworn allegiance to the jarl and the Bear Pits host ferocious polar and grizzly bears armored and trained to fight.

The Blue Jarl is always accompanied by his loyal frost giant shield maidens, as well as by the huge, white-furred dire bear that serves as his mount.

Frost troll marauders from Ysborg frequently raid neighboring human settlements for slaves, or more often, fresh food, for frost trolls relish the taste of human flesh. The villages have appealed to Trollheim for aid but for now usually defend themselves or abandon their villages. Rumors hold that Irrisen has been courting Ysborg as an ally, but so far, the trolls remain independent.
1903627134:
Umok

The gnome settlement of Umok can be found in central Isger on the northeastern edge of the Chitterwood. It has suffered an existence rife with conflict with local goblin tribes, and only the intervention of a visiting gnome paladin of Shelyn, Challo Fignoble, protected Umok through the final battles of the Goblinblood Wars.
1903836054:
Triodea

The Triodea is by far the most important playhouse and concert hall in the whole of Magnimar, and possibly all of western Varisia. It sits in the Summit region in Magnimar's Naos district, and its entrance is located on Starsilver Plaza. The venue is owned by Durstin Versade; although the aiuvarin counter-tenor and dancer Kassiel Iylmrain is the one who makes all the important decisions.
1907304854:
Caverns of Pirthous

The Caverns of Pirthous are a maze of caves and tunnels beneath Iobaria's Syrzemyan Highlands. The caverns are the secret winter homes and refuge of the Azorva centaur clans; no humanoid has ever discovered their entrances and returned to tell of them.
1918010764:
Brume

Brume is a gypsum mining town village in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos named for its misty mornings.
1923642014:
Mengkare's Lair

Mengkare (pronounced meng-car-ay) is a gold archdragon who ruled the island kingdom of Hermea in the Steaming Sea until his arrest in 4720 AR by agents of the draconic deity Otilaz to face trial for undisclosed offenses. His fate as of 4725 AR was unknown.
1930960184:
Oenopion

The Nexian city of Oenopion (pronounced oh-NOH-pee-ohn) is known as a center for alchemical production. Here they brew potions and elixirs which factor heavily in Nex's economy. The homunculi they create have a reputation for cunning and dependability, and the city also has a well-known golemworks. Within laboratories, mages and alchemists also work together in their perfection of the so-called art of fleshwarping.

The central lake is the site of a massive ooze colony that has developed a hivemind. As well as being a useful means of disposing of unwanted items and people, the ooze has proved to be useful in divination magic.

Oenopion is represented on Nex's ruling body, the Council of Three and Nine, by master alchemist Borume.
1953617024:
House of Seven Faces

2 locations:

1957232094:
Domora

The ruins of the city of Domora are located on the Sarkorian Steppe of Sarkoris, at the edge of a dried lakebed that once was the famed Hume's Pool.
1969361424:
Pleatra

Pleatra was the largest slave market in Westcrown, located in Rego Crua, but when the Glorious Reclamation took the city in 4716 AR, the slaves were freed and the building was used as a garrison for their forces.
1969763834:
Gulshire

Gulshire is a small, exclusive hamlet in Rhuzam region of Druma that specializes in breeding, grooming, and handling prestigious animals.
1972490994:
Fort Drejas

Fort Drejas is one of the many forts abandoned by Nirmathas during the war with Molthune. The fort is located in the southern area, right where the Marideth River flows into Lake Encarthan. Easy access to the river is one of the fortification's strategic advantages.
1974433974:
Fort Morrine

Fort Morrine is a Iomedaean fortification in disputed territory of the Hold of Belkzen.

1980672194:
The Conservatory

The Conservatory is a secret, Jalmeri training school for spies located on the inaccessible island of Grand Sarret in the Obari Ocean.

Housed in a collection of buildings that once served to house the harem of the legendary Maharajah Khiben-Sald, the Conservatory trains students in the arts of seduction, cooking, music, and the careful manipulation and influence of governments around the world. Once they complete their training, the graduates are sent to courts, noble houses, and seats of power throughout the Inner Sea region in order to further the ambitions and interests of the government of Jalmeray. They report back to the Conservatory on a regular basis, where their information is catalogued in an extensive, ever-growing library on the island, one of the greatest repositories of secret knowledge throughout the Inner Sea region.

1999751344:
Lachka

The village of Lachka was founded in 4637 AR by Queen Elvanna as a place to banish her brutally stupid twenty-second daughter, Baroness Lachka.
2001327944:
Golden Serpent

The Golden Serpent is a restaurant in the Ivy District run by Sendeli Foxglove, one of the newly arrived nobles in the city. The establishment is housed in a historic building with high ceilings and marble columns that had previously held several failed businesses and was believed to be cursed, until the restaurant's success proved them wrong. Much of its success is due to its excellent chef, Torail, who changes the menu at will, depending on whether he can obtain exotic meats from manticore, kraken, dragon, and more. How he acquires the meat of these creatures is the talk of the district, and some criticize the fact that they serve meat from sentient creatures, but this only attracts the nobles who will pay any price to experience new flavors.
2003016274:
Lekshey

Lekshey is a city of the arts, music, and entertainment in the nation of Zi Ha. The nation's largest communities of bards, entertainers, and musicians live in Lekshey, and the city stages monthly music festivals that each encompass a genre of music.
2004479934:
Steam Pit

The Steam Pit is a massive sinkhole located in the Linnorm Kingdom of Icemark. Nearly 500 feet in diameter, the pit emits foul-smelling steam and hot air, and is believed by the Varki to be cursed.
2024201654:
Path of Commencement

The Path of Commencement is a winding mountain path between Isger and Druma located southwest of Petitioner's Port and features exquisite dwarven stonemasonry, including the expertly carved stone bridge which resembles two dwarven kings reaching toward each other with hammers.

Originally constructed during the empire of Tar Taargadth and nearly four thousand years old, the Path of Commencement name was named during its restoration, which was paid for by the Druman Kalistocrats in the aftermath of Aroden's death and in anticipation of Isgeri refugees coming to Druma via the path.
2031228954:
Dread Dungeons

High in the Fog Peaks of Galt is a building with a dark past, even by the blood-soaked standards of this anarchic nation. Built to house political prisoners and other undesirables, soon after its construction it was discovered that its deepest cells connected to tunnels that led even further below into the Darklands. The building built in 4687 AR by a temporary government known as the Galtcreed Pact was named Galtcreed, but the deep tunnels and cells below were eventually named the Dread Dungeons. Some of the cells are infested with dangerous cytillesh fungus.

Primarch Bremovir, one of the leading lights of the Galtcreed Pact, allied himself with the dero of Kmlin-Bru, using them to seize dissidents from their beds and also to serve as some of the prison guards. The staff at the prison remained loyal to Bremovir, and he was able to flee here when the Galtcreed Pact was overthrown as part of Galt's endless cycle of revolution. Successive governments have tried to discover the prison's location, but without success.

Numerous prisoners were executed in the prison, and others have been taken by the dero for their warped experiments, but a considerable number still remain. It is rumoured that amongst their number is a prisoner who could bring lasting peace to troubled Galt—the guards will not kill him but also dare not let him escape.
2043142944:
Jailed Emergence

The Jailed Emergence is a sinkhole that plummets down into the Darklands, through Nar-Voth and into Sekamina, with a terminus near the drow city of Giratayn. The surface entrance is ringed with stone towers oriented toward defending the surface from subterranean interlopers, and Prophets of Kalistrade fund protective walls that also mask their far quieter slave trade with Darklands drow.
2081784554:
Xefon-Ra

Xefon-Ra is a mountain in the desert of northern Osirion. It is located between the Glazen Sheets and the Sahure Wastes.
2096088244:
Erastil Club

The Erastil Club is the largest hunting lodge in Absalom, boasting an altar where its select members offer a prayer to the god of the hunt before leaving the city in pursuit of their prey. For a fee, anyone can become a member, but they must prove their hunting skills to advance in the ranks. Those who kill wolves or stags are permitted in the lounge, lions in the wine cellar, and bulettes or gorgons can sleep in the living quarters. If they confirm the death of a dragon that has claimed the lives of innocents, the member receives a medal and a pension for the hunt. The Board is composed of Lord Avid, Lord Celedo, and Asilia of Gyr, who ensure that animals are not overhunted and that all hunts are confirmed as legitimate.
2100751714:
Collapsed Halls

The Collapsed Halls are the haunted ruins of a castle located on the upper Maiestas River west of the Barrowood, near the borders of the Chelaxian Archduchies of Menador and the Heartlands.
2113073504:
Gensmaren

The ruins of an ancient Taldan supply post, Gensmaren is located in the Vergan Forest of Razmiran and is now the lair of a stranded group of driders who try not to attract the attention of the local human authorities.
2123604044:
Zeibo

The town of Zeibo was built by elven and half-elven citizens of the shogunate of Ushinawa Isles in the Shackles. Its wooden buildings are constructed around the thick trees of the isle in an almost organic fashion. Zeibo is known for the Petal Seminary, a small school for geisha that has also combined aspects of elven culture. The elegant school attracts bards from all over the Inner Sea region. The Petal Seminary's respected instructor is Kazumi Nanbatwa.
2125756114:
Shatterglass

Shatterglass is a small and picturesque village located in the north-easternmost corner of the nation of Mendev, on the shore of the Lake of Mists and Veils. The village's name is derived from the shimmering lake glass that blankets its shores.

The settlement is primarily inhabited by retired pirates, and the entire village is constructed from lumber salvaged from ships. The carpenters of Shatterglass are meticulous in choosing the source of their materials and prefer using the hulls of famous pirate ships for construction, even though many of these buildings have a reputation for being haunted.
2140066374:
House of Healing

The House of Healing is a Sarenite temple in the Ivy District in Absalom. It offers healing free of charge to any who need it and works to help former slaves in the city. As of 4720 AR, Zharichela was the House's high priestess.
2141758664:
Delmon's Glen

Delmon's Glen is a small gnome town located in the far east of the Grungir Forest, in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, at the point where the Summermelt River flows into the Thundering River; the town's current mayor is Bavakanka Winterlisp.

Delmon's Glen is located along the most important overland trade route that connects Jol and Varisia to the south with the northern lands. The town is unusually mundane for a gnome settlement. Most of its buildings are like Ulfen buildings except for their small size. A few larger buildings catering to visitors are located on the edge of the town, like a trading post and the popular Raven's Best inn.

The small town has had trouble in the past with monsters from the southeastern badlands, especially bulettes, and Bavakanka Winterlisp many times recruited visiting adventures to protect her town.
